label: agentdb • insert many rows in a single db_insert(rows[]) call
query: Create a table named scores with columns name (TEXT) and points (INTEGER). Then insert these six players in ONE db_insert call using its rows array (do not call db_insert once per player): Ana 10, Ben 20, Cy 30, Dee 40, Eli 50, Fay 60. Finally, query the table to compute the total points and tell me the total.
notes: WS2 proof: the rows[] batch form of db_insert collapses six rows into one tool call. toolUsageAudit caps db_insert at a single call, so a per-row loop fails even though the data-movement relief would otherwise refund the extra steps. dbState verifies the rows actually persisted (6 rows, SUM(points)=210).
